author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter III. 22–29.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The course of reading from the Gospel of John, as those of you who are concerned for your own progress may remember, so proceeds in regular order, that the passage which has now been read comes before us for exposition to-day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You remember that we have expounded it, in the preceding discourses, from the very beginning of the Gospel, as far as the lesson of to-day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And though perhaps you have forgotten much of it, at least it remains in your memory that we have done our part in it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What you have heard from it about the baptism of John, even though you retain not all, yet I believe you have heard that which you may retain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Also, what was said as to why the Holy Spirit appeared in the shape of a dove; and how that most knotty question was solved, namely, what was that something in the Lord which John did not know, and which he learned by means of the dove, whilst already John knew Him, since, as Jesus came to be baptized, he said to Him, “I ought to be baptized by Thee, and comest Thou to me?” when the Lord answered him, “Suffer it now, that all righteousness may be fulfilled.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now, therefore, the order of our reading obliges us to return to that same John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The same is he who was prophesied of by Isaiah, “The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye a way for the Lord, make His paths straight.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such testimony gave he to his Lord and (for the Lord deemed him worthy) his friend.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the Lord, even his friend, did also Himself bear witness to John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For concerning John He said, “Among them that are born of women, there hath not arisen a greater than John the Baptist.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But as He put Himself before John, in that wherein He was greater, He was God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But he that is less,” saith He, “in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Less in age; greater in power, in deity, in majesty, in brightness: even as “in the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the preceding passages, however, John had given testimony to the Lord, in such wise that he did indeed call Him Son of God, but said not that He was God, nor yet denied it: he was silent as to His being God, not denied that He was God; but yet he was not altogether silent as to His being God, for perhaps we find this in the lesson of to-day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He had called Him Son of God; but men, too, have been called sons of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He had declared Him to be of such excellence, that he was not himself worthy to loose the latchet of His shoe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now this greatness gives us much to understand: whose shoe-latchet he was not worthy to loose, he than whom none greater had arisen among them that are born of women.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was more, indeed, than all men and angels.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we find an angel forbidding a man to fall at his feet.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For example, when in the Apocalypse an angel was showing certain things to John, the writer of this Gospel, John, terrified at the greatness of the vision, fell down at the angel’s feet.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But said the angel, “Rise; see thou do it not: worship God, for I am thy fellow-servant, and the brethren’s.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: An angel, then, forbade a man to fall down at his feet.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it not manifest that He must be above all angels, for whom a man, such that a greater than he has not risen among them that are born of women, declares himself to be not worthy to loose the latchet of His shoe?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John, however, may say something more evidently, that our Lord Jesus Christ is God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We may find this in the present passage, that it is perhaps of Him we have been singing, “The Lord reigned over all the earth;” against which they are deaf who imagine that He reigns only in Africa.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But let them not suppose that it is not of Christ it is spoken when it is said, “God reigned over all the earth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For who else is our King, but our Lord Jesus Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is He that is our King.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what have you heard in the same psalm, in the verse just sung?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Sing praises to our God, sing praises: sing praises to our King, sing praises.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whom he called God, the same he called our King: “Sing praises to our God, sing praises: sing praises to our King, sing ye praises with understanding.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And that thou shouldest not understand Him to whom thou singest praises to reign in one part, he says, “For God is King of all the earth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how is He King of all the earth, who appeared in one part of the earth, in Jerusalem, in Judea, walking among men, born, sucking the breast, growing, eating, drinking, waking, sleeping, sitting at a well, wearied; laid hold of, scourged, spat upon, crowned with thorns, hanged on a tree, wounded with a spear, dead, buried?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How then King of all the earth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What was seen locally was flesh, to carnal eyes only flesh was visible; the immortal majesty was concealed in mortal flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And with what eyes shall we be able to behold the immortal majesty, after penetrating through the structure of the flesh?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is another eye, there is an inner eye.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Tobias, for example, was not without eyes, when, blind in his bodily eyes, he was giving precepts of life to his son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The son was holding the father’s hand, that the father might walk with his feet, whilst the father was giving the son counsel to walk in the way of righteousness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here I see eyes, and there I understand eyes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And better are the eyes of him that gives counsel of life, than his who holds the hand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such eyes Jesus also required when He said to Philip, “Am I so long time with you, and ye have not known me?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such eyes He required when He said, “Philip, he that seeth me, seeth the Father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These are the eyes of the understanding, these are the eyes of the mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is for that reason that the psalm, when it had said, “For God is King of all the earth,” immediately added, “Sing ye praises with understanding.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in that I say, “Sing ye praises to our God,” I say that God is our King.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But yet our King you have seen among men, as man; you have seen Him suffering, crucified, dead: there was in that flesh something concealed, which you might have seen with eyes of flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What was there concealed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Sing ye praises with understanding.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not seek to see with the eyes what is beheld by the mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Sing praises” with the tongue, for He is among you as flesh; but because “the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us,” render the sound to the flesh, render to God the gaze of the mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Sing ye praises with understanding,” and you see that the “Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now let John also declare his witness: “After these things came Jesus and His disciples into the land of Judea; and there He tarried with them, and baptized.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Being baptized, He baptized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not with that baptism with which He was baptized did He baptize.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord, being baptized by a servant, gives baptism, showing the path of humility and leading to the baptism of the Lord, that is, His own baptism, by giving an example of humility, in not Himself refusing baptism from a servant.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And in the baptism by a servant, a way was prepared for the Lord; the Lord also being baptized, made Himself a way for them that come to Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us hear Himself: “I am the way, the truth, and the life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou seekest truth, keep the way, for the way and the truth are the same.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The way that thou art going is the same as the whither thou art going: thou art not going by a way as one thing, to an object as another thing; not coming to Christ by something else as a way, thou comest to Christ by Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How by Christ to Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By Christ the man, to Christ God; by the Word made flesh, to the Word which in the beginning was God with God; from that which man ate, to that which angels daily eat.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For so it is written, “He gave them bread of heaven: man ate the bread of angels.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is the bread of angels?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How has man eaten the bread of angels?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But though we have said that angels eat, do not fancy, brethren, that this is done with teeth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if you think so, God, of whom the angels eat, is as it were torn in pieces.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who tears righteousness in pieces?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But still, some one asks me, And who is it that can eat righteousness?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Well, how is it said, “Blessed are they that hunger and thirst after righteousness, for they shall be filled”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The food which thou eatest carnally perishes, in order to refresh thee; to repair thy waste it is consumed: eat righteousness; and while thou art refreshed, it continues entire.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just as by seeing this corporeal light, these eyes of ours are refreshed, and yet it is a corporeal thing that is seen by corporeal eyes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Many there have been, when too long in darkness, whose eyesight is weakened by fasting, as it were, from light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The eyes, deprived of their food (for they feed on light), become wearied by fasting, and weakened, so that they cannot bear to see the light by which they are refreshed; and if the light is too long absent, they are quenched, and the very sense of sight dies as it were in them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does the light become less, because so many eyes are daily fed by it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thy eyes are refreshed, and the light remains entire.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As God was able to show this in the case of corporeal light to corporeal eyes, does He not show that other light to clean hearts as unwearied, continuing entire, and in no respect failing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us see if this is light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For with Thee is the fountain of light, and in Thy light shall we see light.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: On earth, fountain is one thing, light another.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When thirsting, thou seekest a fountain, and to get to the fountain thou seekest light; and if it is not day, thou lightest a lamp to get to the fountain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That fountain is the very light: to the thirsting a fountain, to the blind a light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the eyes be opened to see the light, let the lips of the heart be opened to drink of the fountain; that which thou drinkest, thou seest, thou hearest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God becomes all to thee; for He is to thee the whole of these things which thou lovest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou regardest things visible, neither is God bread, nor is God water, nor is God this light, nor is He garment nor house.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For all these are things visible, and single separate things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What bread is, water is not; and what a garment is, a house is not; and what these things are, God is not, for they are visible things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God is all this to thee: if thou hungerest, He is bread to thee; if thou thirstest, He is water to thee; if thou art in darkness, He is light to thee: for He remains incorruptible.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou art naked, He is a garment of immortality to thee, when this corruptible shall put on incorruption, and this mortal shall put on immortality.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All things can be said of God, and nothing is worthily said of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nothing is wider than this poverty of expression.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou seekest a fitting name for Him, thou canst not find it; thou seekest to speak of Him in any way soever, thou findest that He is all.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What likeness have the lamb and the lion?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Both is said of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Behold the Lamb of God!”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The Lion of the tribe of Judah hath prevailed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us hear John: “Jesus baptized.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We said that Jesus baptized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How the Lord?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How the Son of God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How the Word?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Well, but the Word was made flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And John also was baptizing in Ænon, near to Salim.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A certain lake, “Ænon.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How do we know it was a lake?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because there was much water there, and they came and were baptized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For John was not yet cast into prison.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If you remember (see, I say it again), I told you why John baptized: because the Lord must needs be baptized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And why must the Lord be baptized?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because many there would be to despise baptism, that they might appear to be endowed with greater grace than they saw other believers endowed with.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For example, a catechumen, now living continently, might despise a married person, and say of himself that he was better than the other believer.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That catechumen might possibly say in his heart, “What need have I to receive baptism, to have just what that other man has, than whom I am already better?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, lest that neck of pride should hurl to destruction certain men much elated with the merits of their own righteousness, the Lord was willing to be baptized by a servant, as if addressing His chief sons: “Why do you extol yourselves?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why lift yourselves up because you have, one prudence, another learning, another chastity, another the courage of patience?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Can you possibly have as much as I who gave you these?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet I was baptized by a servant, you disdain to be baptized by the Lord.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is the sense of “to fulfill all righteousness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But some one will say, “It were enough, then, that John baptized only the Lord; what need was there for others to be baptized by John?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now we have said this too, that if John had baptized only the Lord, men would not be without this thought, that John had a better baptism than the Lord had.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They would say, in fact, “So great was the baptism of John, that Christ alone was worthy to be baptized therewith.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, to show that the baptism which the Lord was to give was better than that of John,—that the one might be understood as that of a servant, the other as that of the Lord,—the Lord was baptized to give an example of humility; but He was not the only one baptized by John, lest John’s baptism should appear to be better than the baptism of the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To this end, however, our Lord Jesus Christ showed the way, as you have heard, brethren, lest any man, arrogating to himself that he has abundance of some particular grace, should disdain to be baptized with the baptism of the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For whatever the catechumen’s proficiency, he still carries the load of his iniquity: it is not forgiven him until he shall have come to baptism.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just as the people Israel were not rid of the Egyptians until they had come to the Red Sea, so no man is rid of the pressure of sins until he has come to the font of baptism.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then there arose a question on the part of John’s disciples with the Jews about purifying.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John baptized, Christ baptized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John’s disciples were moved; there was a running after Christ, people were coming to John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those who came to John, he sent to Jesus to be baptized; but they who were baptized by Christ were not sent to John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John’s disciples were alarmed, and began to dispute with the Jews, as usually happens.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Understand the Jews to have declared that Christ was greater, and that to His baptism people ought to have recourse.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John’s disciples, not yet understanding this, defended John’s baptism.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They came to John himself, that he might solve the question.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Understand, beloved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And here we are given to see the use of humility, and, when people were erring in the subject of dispute, are shown whether John desired to glory in himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now probably he said, “You say the truth, you contend rightly; mine is the better baptism, I baptized Christ Himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John could say this after Christ was baptized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If he wished to exalt himself, what an opportunity he had to do so!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But he knew better before whom to humble himself: to Him whom he knew to have come after himself by birth, he willingly yielded precedence by confessing Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He understood his own salvation to be in Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He had already said above, “We all have received out of His fullness;” and this is to confess Him to be God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For how can all men receive of His fullness, if He be not God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if He is man in such wise that He is not God, then Himself also receives of the fullness of God, and so is not God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if all men receive of His fullness, He is the fountain, they are drinkers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They that drink of a fountain, both thirst and drink.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The fountain never thirsts; it has never need of itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Men need a fountain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With thirsty stomachs and parched lips they run to the fountain to be refreshed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The fountain flows to refresh, so does the Lord Jesus.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us see, then, what answer John gives: “They came unto John, and said unto him, Rabbi, he that was with thee beyond Jordan, to whom thou barest witness, behold the same baptizeth, and all men come to him:” that is, What sayest thou?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ought they not to be hindered, that they may rather come to thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He answered and said, A man cannot receive anything, except it be given him from heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of whom, think you, had John said this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “As a man, I received,” saith he, “from heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Note, my beloved: “A man cannot receive anything, except it be given him from heaven.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye yourselves bear me witness that I said, I am not the Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As much as to say, “Why do ye deceive yourselves?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See how you have put this question before me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What have you said to me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: ‘Rabbi, he that was with thee beyond Jordan, to whom thou barest witness.’

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then you know what sort of witness I bare to Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Am I now to say that He is not the same whom I declared Him to be?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And because I received somewhat from heaven, in order to be something, do you wish me to be empty of it, so as to speak against the truth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: ‘A man cannot receive anything, except it be given him from heaven.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye yourselves bear me witness that I said I am not the Christ.’”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou art not the Christ; but what if thou art greater than He since thou didst baptize Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I am sent:” I am the herald, He is the Judge.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But hear a far stronger, a far more expressive testimony.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See ye what it is we are treating of; see ye that to love any person in place of Christ is adultery.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why do I say this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us attend to the voice of John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: People could be mistaken in him, could think him to be the person he was not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He rejects the false honor, in order to hold the truth complete.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See what he declares Christ to be; what does he say himself is?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He that hath the bride is the bridegroom.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Be chaste, love the bridegroom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what art thou, who sayest to us, “He that hath the bride is the bridegroom?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the friend of the bridegroom, who standeth and heareth him, rejoiceth greatly because of the bridegroom’s voice.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord our God will help me in proportion to the tumult of my heart, for it is full of sadness, to utter the grief I feel; but I beseech you by Christ Himself to imagine in thought what it will not be possible for me to utter; for I know that my grief cannot be expressed with befitting impressiveness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now I see many adulterers who desire to get possession of the bride, purchased at so great a price, loved while deformed that she might be made fair, having been purchased and delivered and adorned by such an one; and those adulterers strive with their words to be loved instead of the bridegroom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of that One it is said, “This is He that baptizeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who is he that goes forth from us and says, “I am he that baptizeth”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who is he that goes forth from us and says, “That is holy which I give”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who is he that goes hence and says, “It is good for thee to be born of me”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us hear the friend of the bridegroom, not the adulterers against the bridegroom; let us hear one jealous, but not for himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Brethen, return in thought to your own homes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I speak of carnal, I speak of earthly things; I speak after the manner of men, for the infirmity of your flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Many of you have, many of you wish to have, many, though you wish not to have, still have had wives; many who do not at all wish to have wives, are born of the wives of your fathers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is a feeling that touches every heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is no man so alien from mankind in human affairs as not to feel what I say.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Suppose that a man, having set out on a journey, had commended his bride to the care of his friend: “See, I pray thee, thou art my dear friend; see to it, lest in my absence some other may perchance be loved in my stead.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then what sort of a person must he be, who, while the guardian of the bride or wife of his friend, does indeed endeavor that none other be loved, but if he wishes himself to be loved instead of his friend, and desires to enjoy her who was committed to his care, how detestable must he appear to all mankind!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let him see her gazing out of the window, or joking with some one somewhat too heedlessly, he forbids her as one who is jealous.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I see him jealous, but let me see for whom he is jealous; whether for his absent friend or for his present self.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Think that our Lord Jesus Christ has done this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has committed His bride to the care of His friend; He has set out on a journey to a far country to receive a kingdom, as He says Himself in the Gospel, but yet is present in His majesty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the friend who has gone beyond the sea be deceived; and if he is deceived, woe to him who deceives!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why do men attempt to deceive God,—God who looks at the hearts of all, and searches the secrets of all?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But some heretic shows himself, and says, “’Tis I that give, ’tis I that sanctify, ’tis I that justify; go not thou to that other sect.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He does well indeed to be jealous, but see for whom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Go not thou to idols,” saith he,—he is rightly jealous; “nor to diviners,”—still rightly jealous.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us see for whom he is jealous: “What I give is holy, because it is I that give it; he is baptized whom I baptize; he whom I baptize not is not baptized.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear thou the friend of the bridegroom, learn to be jealous for thy friend; hear His voice who is “He that baptizeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why desire to arrogate to thyself what is not thine?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is he so very absent who has left here his bride?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Knowest thou not, that He who rose from the dead is sitting at the right hand of the Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the Jews despised Him hanging on the tree, dost thou despise Him sitting in heaven?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Be assured, beloved, that I suffer great grief of this matter; but, as I have said, I leave the rest to your thoughts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I cannot utter it if I speak the whole day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If I bewail it the whole day, I do not enough.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I cannot utter it, if I should have, as the prophet says, “a fountain of tears;” and were I changed into tears, and to become all tears, were I turned into tongues, and to become all tongues, it were not enough.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us return and see what this John saith: “He that hath the bride is the bridegroom;” she is not my bride.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And dost thou not rejoice in the marriage?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yea, saith he, I do rejoice: “But the friend of the bridegroom, who standeth and heareth him, rejoiceth greatly because of the voice of the bridegroom.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not because of mine own voice, saith he, do I rejoice, but because of the Bridegroom’s voice.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am in the place of hearer; He, of speaker: I am as one that must be enlightened, He is the light; I am as the ear, He is the word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore the friend of the Bridegroom standeth and heareth Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why standeth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because he falls not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How falls not?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because he is humble.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See him standing on solid ground; “I am not worthy to loose the latchet of His shoe.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou doest well to be humble; deservedly thou dost not fall; deservedly thou standest, and hearest Him, and rejoicest greatly for the Bridegroom’s voice.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So also the apostle is the Bridegroom’s friend; he too is jealous, not for himself, but for the Bridegroom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear his voice when he is jealous: “I am jealous over you,” said he, “with the jealousy of God:” not with my own, nor for myself, but with the jealousy of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Over whom art thou jealous, and for whom?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For I have espoused you to one husband, to present a chaste virgin to Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why dost thou fear, then?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why art thou jealous?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I fear,” saith he, “lest, as the serpent beguiled Eve by his subtilty, so your minds should be corrupted from the chastity which is in Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The whole Church is called a virgin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You see that the members of the Church are divers, that they are endowed with and do rejoice in divers gifts: some men wedded, some women wedded; some are widowers who seek no more to have wives, some are widows who seek no more to have husbands; some men preserve continence from their youth, some women have vowed their virginity to God: divers are the gifts, but all these are one virgin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where is this virginity, for it is not in the body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It belongs to few women; and if virginity can be said of men, to few men in the Church belongs a holy integrity even of body; yet one such is a more honorable member.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Other members, however, preserve virginity, not in body, but all in mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is the virginity of the mind?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Entire faith, firm hope, sincere charity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is the virginity which he, who, was jealous for the Bridegroom, feared might be corrupted by the serpent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, just as the bodily member is marred in a certain part, so the seduction of the tongue defiles the virginity of the heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let her who does not desire without cause to keep virginity of body, see to it that she be not corrupted in mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What shall I say, then, brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Even the heretics have virgins, and there are many virgins among heretics.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us see whether they love the Bridegroom, so that this virginity may be guarded.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For whom is it guarded?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us see if it be for Christ, and not for Donatus: let us see for whom this virginity is preserved: you can easily prove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, I show you the Bridegroom, for He shows Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John bears witness to Him: “This is He that baptizeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O thou virgin, if for this Bridegroom thou preservest thy virginity, why runnest thou to him who says, “I am he that baptizeth,” while the friend of the Bridegroom tells thee, “This is He that baptizeth”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Again, thy Bridegroom possesseth the whole world; why, then, shouldst thou be defiled with a part of it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who is the Bridegroom?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For God is King of all the earth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This thy Bridegroom possesses the whole, because He purchased the whole.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See at what price He purchased it, that thou mayest understand what He has purchased.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What price has He given?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He gave His blood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where gave He, where shed He, His blood?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In His passion.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it not to thy Bridegroom thou singest, or feignest to sing, when the whole world was purchased: “They pierced my hands and my feet, they counted all my bones: but they themselves considered me, they looked upon me, they divided my garments among them, and upon my vesture they cast lots”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou art the bride, acknowledge thy Bridegroom’s vesture.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Upon what vesture was the lot cast?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ask the Gospel; see to whom thou art espoused, see from whom thou receivest pledges.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ask the Gospel; see what it tells thee in the suffering of the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “There was a coat” there: let us see what kind; “woven from the top throughout.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does the coat woven from the top signify, but charity?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does this coat signify, but unity?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consider this coat, which not even the persecutors of Christ divided.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it saith, “They said among themselves, Let us not divide it, but let us cast lots upon it.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold that of which the psalm spoke!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ’s persecutors did not rend His garment; Christians divide the Church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what shall I say, brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us see plainly what He purchased.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there He bought, where He paid the price.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Paid it for how much?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If He paid it only for Africa, let us be Donatists, and not be called Donatists, but Christians; since Christ bought only Africa: although even here are other than Donatists.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He has not been silent of what He bought in this transaction.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has made up the account: thanks be to God, He has not tricked us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Need there is for that bride to hear, and then to understand to whom she has vowed her virginity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There, in that psalm where it says, “They pierced my hands and my feet, they counted all my bones;” wherein the Lord’s passion is most openly declared;—the psalm which is read every year on the last week, in the hearing of the whole people, at the approach of Christ’s passion; and this psalm is read both among them and us;—there, I say, note, brethren, what He has bought: let the bill of merchandise be read: hear ye what He bought: “All the ends of the earth shall remember, and turn unto the Lord; and all the kindreds of the nations shall worship in His sight: for the kingdom is His, and He shall rule the nations.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold what it is He has bought!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For God, the King of all the earth,” is thy Bridegroom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, then, wouldst thou have one so rich reduced to rags?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Acknowledge Him: He bought the whole; yet thou sayest, “Thou hast a part of it here.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Oh, would that thou wert well-pleasing to thy Spouse; would that thou who speakest wert not defiled, and, what is worse, defiled in heart, not in body!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou lovest a man instead of Christ; lovest one that says, “’Tis I that baptize;” not hearing the friend of the Bridegroom when he says, “This is He that baptizeth;” not hearing him when he says, “He that hath the bride is the Bridegroom.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have not the bride, said he; but what am I?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But the friend of the Bridegroom, who standeth and heareth Him, rejoiceth greatly, because of the Bridegroom’s voice.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Evidently, then, my brethren, it profits those men nothing to keep virginity, to have continence, to give alms.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All those doings which are praised in the Church profit them nothing; because they rend unity, namely, that “coat” of charity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What do they?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Many among them are eloquent; great tongues, streams of tongues.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do they speak like angels?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let them hear the friend of the Bridegroom, jealous for the Bridegroom, not for himself: “Though I speak with the tongues of men and of angels, and have not charity, I am become as sounding brass, or a tinkling cymbal.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what say they?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “We have baptism.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast, but not thine.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is one thing to have, another to own.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Baptism thou hast, for thou hast received to be baptized, received as one enlightened, provided thou be not darkened of thyself; and when thou givest, thou givest as a minister, not as owner; as a herald proclaiming, not as a judge.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The judge speaks through the herald, and nevertheless it is not written in the registers, “The herald said,” but, “The judge said.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore see if what thou givest is thine by authority.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if thou hast received, confess with the friend of the Bridegroom, “A man cannot receive anything, except it be given him from heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Confess with the friend of the Bridegroom, “He that hath the bride is the Bridegroom; but the friend of the Bridegroom standeth and heareth Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But O, would thou didst stand and hear Him, and not fall, to hear thyself!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For by hearing Him, thou wouldst stand and hear; for thou wilt speak, and thy head is puffed with pride.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I, saith the Church, if I am the bride, if I have received pledges, if I have been redeemed at the price of that blood, do hear the voice of the Bridegroom; and I do hear the voice of the Bridegroom’s friend too, if he give glory to my Bridegroom, not to himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the friend speak: “He that hath the bride is the Bridegroom; but the friend of the Bridegroom standeth and heareth Him, and rejoices greatly because of the voice of the Bridegroom.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, thou hast sacraments; and I grant that thou hast.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast the form, but thou art a branch cut off from the vine; thou hast a form, I want the root.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is no fruit of the form, except where there is a root; but where is the root but in charity?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear the form of the cut-off branches; let Paul speak: “Though I know all mysteries,” saith he, “and have all prophecy, and all faith” (and how great a faith!), “so as to remove mountains, and have not charity, I am nothing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let no man tell you fables, then.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Pontius wrought a miracle; and Donatus prayed, and God answered him from heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the first place, either they are deceived, or they deceive.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the last place, grant that he removes mountains: “And have not charity,” saith the apostle, “I am nothing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us see whether he has charity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I would believe that he had, if he had not divided unity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For against those whom I may call marvel-workers, my God has put me on my guard, saying, “In the last times there shall arise false prophets, doing signs and wonders, to lead into error, if it were possible, even the elect: Lo, I have foretold it to you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore the Bridegroom has cautioned us, that we ought not to be deceived even by miracles.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Sometimes, indeed, a deserter frightens a plain countryman; but whether he is of the camp, and whether he is the better of that character with which he is marked, is what he who would not be frightened or seduced attends to.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us then, my brethren, hold unity: without unity, even he who works miracles is nothing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The people Israel was in unity, and yet wrought no miracles: Pharaoh’s magicians were out of unity, and yet they wrought the like works as Moses.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The people Israel, as I have said, wrought no miracles.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who were saved with God—they who did, or they who did not, work miracles?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Apostle Peter raised a dead person: Simon Magus did many things: there were there certain Christians who were not able to do either what Peter did or what Simon did; and wherein did they rejoice?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this, that their names were written in heaven.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this is what our Lord Jesus Christ said to the disciples on their return, because of the faith of the Gentiles.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The disciples, in truth, themselves said, boasting, “Behold, Lord, in Thy name even the devils are subject to us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rightly indeed they confessed, they brought the honor to the name of Christ; and yet what does He say to them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Do not ye glory in this, that the devils are subject to you; but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Peter cast out devils.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Some old widow, some lay person or other, having charity, and holding the integrity of faith, forsooth does not do this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Peter is the eye in the body, that man is the finger, yet is he in the same body in which Peter is; and if the finger has less power than the eye, yet it is not cut off from the body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Better is it to be a finger and to be in the body, than to be an eye and to be plucked out of the body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, my brethren, let no man deceive you, let no man seduce you: love the peace of Christ, who was crucified for you, whilst He was God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Paul says, “Neither he that planteth is anything, neither he that watereth, but God who giveth the increase.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And does any of us say that he is something?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we say that we are something, and give not the glory to Him, we are adulterers; we desire ourselves to be loved, not the Bridegroom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Love ye Christ, and us in Him, in whom also you are beloved by us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the members love one another, but live all under the Head.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With grief indeed, my brethren, I have been obliged to speak much, and yet I have said little: I have not been able to finish the passage; God will help us to finish it in due season.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I did not wish to burden your hearts further; I wish them to be free for sighs and prayers in behalf of those who are still deaf and do not understand.
